name: Kai Pad Prik Haeng-Chicken W/Chili And
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Tua fak yao (long beans), cut into 1-inch pieces
1/4 cup Prik haeng (dried red chilis), crumbled
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a wok or skillet on medium heat and carefully toast the uncooked peanuts until they begin to turn golden, and are just cooked through (test by biting one). In a mortar and pestle or food processor, briefly pound the peanuts to produce a broken consistency (not peanut butter!).
2. Heat the wok or skillet over high heat, and add a little peanut oil. When it is hot, saute the garlic until it is golden brown and slightly crispy; remove it and drain on a kitchen towel.
3. Saute the chilis briefly, then add the chicken and continue stirring until it begins to change color.
4. Working quickly, add the remaining ingredients in turn, stirring to mix. Add the soy sauces (Nam pla, Siiew khao, and Siiew dhum) and fish sauce, then finally the stock after the dry ingredients; this will cool the mixture to allow the cooking to finish.
5. Return the garlic to the pan, cover, and leave for about a minute to complete cooking.
6. Check that the meat is cooked, and taste for seasoning balance. Serve with white steamed rice and the usual table condiments.
